question_html: Explain the proverb, “Actions speak louder than words.”
solution_html:
The proverb "Actions speak louder than words" means that what people do shows their true character and intentions more clearly than what they say. Words can be empty or deceptive, but actions reveal sincerity, honesty, and real commitment.

This proverb encourages people to prove their promises through deeds and reminds us that genuine effort and behavior carry more weight than mere talk.

Marks Distribution:

For mentioning that actions reveal true character more than words; 01 mark.

For explaining that deeds prove sincerity and real commitment; 01 mark.

For mentioning that genuine effort carries more weight than mere talk; 0.5 mark.

[Instructions: 0.5 mark will be deducted for two or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]

question_html:
Complete the following sentences:

(a) Where there is a will, ______.

(b) If you had told me earlier, ______.

(c) The man who helped me yesterday ______.

(d) She walks carefully lest ______.

(e) I am accustomed to ______.
solution_html:
(a) Where there is a will, there is a way.

(b) If you had told me earlier, I could have helped you. /I would have helped you.

(c) The man who helped me yesterday is my uncle.

(d) She walks carefully lest she should slip.

(e) I am accustomed to getting up early in the morning.

Marks Distribution:

For completing the given sentences; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

[N.B: Any kind of sentence that is correct, will also get allocated marks.]

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘The Role of Youth in Climate Action’ within 100 words.
solution_html:
The Role of Youth in Climate Action

Youth play a crucial role in addressing the climate crisis as they are energetic, innovative, and aware of the environmental challenges facing the world. In Bangladesh, young people are leading campaigns to reduce pollution, promote tree planting, and raise awareness about climate change. They participate in community programs, social media movements, and educational projects that encourage sustainable practices. By adopting eco-friendly habits, influencing policy decisions, and inspiring others, youth can create a significant impact on environmental protection. Governments and organizations must support young activists with training, resources, and opportunities to implement their ideas. Engaging youth in climate action not only benefits the environment but also builds responsible citizens who will continue to advocate for a sustainable future.

Marks Distribution:

For writing the title “The Role of Youth in Climate Action”: 0.5 mark

For explaining the importance of youth in climate initiatives: 01 mark

For mentioning their activities such as campaigns, awareness, and projects: 01 mark

For suggesting ways to support youth including training, resources, and opportunities: 1.5 marks

For concluding with the impact on environment and responsible citizenship: 01 mark

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* Students must write it within 100 words limit. For exceeding the 100 words limit, 0.5 marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
